LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-31-2023 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT STEPHEN SETO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MEET AND CONFER WAS INADEQUATE AND DOES NOT COMPLY WITH 430.41(A)(1). THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND THE RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY FEBRUARY 28, 2023. IN ADDITION, A NEW DECLARATION MUST THEN BE FILED WHICH SPECIFIES WHETHER THE MEET AND CONFER WAS IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-31-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DANIEL L. GATTO, Esq. of Gomez Trial Attorneys (Tel: 619-237-3490) for plaintiff Jane Doe;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT STEPHEN SETO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

The Court notes that plaintiff did not properly contest the Court's tentative ruling, and that the opposing side is not present.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The meet and confer was inadequate and does not comply with 430.41(a)(1). The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and the response to the complaint is now due by February 28, 2023. In addition, a new declaration must then be filed which specifies whether the meet and confer was in person or by telephone.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-03-2023 Continued To Mar-14-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal For Delay In Prosecution; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Nov-14-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STEPHEN SETO's Motion For Dismissal For Delay In Prosecution is denied.

"In computing the time within which service must be made pursuant to this article, there shall be excluded the time during which any of the following conditions existed:
(a) The defendant was not amenable to the process of the court.
(b) The prosecution of the action or proceedings in the action was stayed and the stay affected service.
(c) The validity of service was the subject of litigation by the parties.
(d) Service, for any other reason, was impossible, impracticable, or futile due to causes beyond the plaintiff's control. Failure to discover relevant facts or evidence is not a cause beyond the plaintiff's control for the purpose of this subdivision." (CCP 583.240.)

Here, Plaintiff filed her complaint on June 21, 2019. Service was completed on September 1, 2022, and proof was filed with this Court on September 12, 2022. The parties agree that there is tolling under CCP 583.240(c). Plaintiff is entitled to a 59 day extension, due to the time period when the validity of service was the subject of litigation by the parties. Thus, Plaintiff needed to serve Defendant by August 18, 2022. However, under sec. 583.240(d), service was impracticable for a period of time in 2020, because of the impact of the Covid 19 pandemic on the courts. (Opp. 7-8.) Specifically, Plaintiff filed an application for publication of summons in Dept. 610 on March 10, 2020, at the onset of the Covid 19 pandemic, and did not receive a ruling until August 3, 2022, which made service impracticable due to causes beyond her control. Further, Plaintiff made diligent efforts to serve Defendant, including hiring a company to skip trace, attempting to serve at his business, attempts to serve at the taproom he was associated with, service attempts at the home address he was associated with, contacting an attorney that previously litigated against Defendant, sending a notice of acknowledgment of receipt, a stakeout of the Grant St. address where a maintenance worker confirmed Defendant was living, attempting service at the Ritz Carlton, hiring an investigator (Morse 7/19/21 Decl., 2-3), sending another notice of acknowledgement of receipt to Plaintiff and his attorney, and requesting Defendant's attorney provide his address for service-which Defendant's attorney declined to provide (Garrard 6/10/22 Decl., 4-6). Therefore, the case will be decided on the merits.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-08-2022 Continued To May-24-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Mar-04-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Richard Morse, Esq. (619.684.3092; rich@morseinjurylaw.com) of Morse Injury Law for Plaintiff.
C. Alex Naegele, Esq. (408.883.8994; alex@canlawcorp.com) for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT STEPHEN SETO'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is granted. Plaintiff Jane Doe has failed to properly serve the summons and complaint on Defendant. Each time Plaintiff has tried to serve Defendant, she has failed to check any boxes as to how Defendant was served in the "NOTICE TO THE PERSON SERVED" section. This is not substantial compliance with the requirements of the Code of Civil Procedure. (Plaintiff Opp. Exhibit A; Carol Gilbert, Inc. v. Haller (2009) 179 Cal.App.4th 852, 866.) Further, the proof of service of the summons was served on a person not authorized to accept service on Defendant's behalf. Finally, Plaintiff does not address the service issue but instead contends that the motion is untimely. This argument is moot as the Court finds this motion was made at the time of Defendant's first appearance. For all these reasons, the Court has not acquired jurisdiction over Defendant.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
